form_answers.question: Is Household-Related Items approved in the budget?
Is the item related to a need or goal identified in the Life Plan?
Will the item increase independence, or substitute for human assistance?
Does the item increase safety of the participant in their home environment?
Is the item exclusively needed for the participant?
Does the item increase the participant’s health and safety?
Does the individual live in their own home? (for Household support only)
Was the same item bought already in this budget year?
Can the item be funded through other sources?

request.justification: Esther would like a laptop to access online classes and communicate with family.
request.extraction_notes: Safety features: N/A No 'Name of Provider/Vendor' label on this form; the provider/vendor name could not be read (the item link points to 'amazon.com').

findings.criterion_text: The item exists at the linked page, with a visible price
findings.note: Could not confirm the item and price on the page — please review the listing.

findings.criterion_text: Item is plausibly NOT on the HRI exclusion list (not a cell phone, computer, vehicle, medical device, pill dispenser)
findings.note: The requested item matches an exclusion-list keyword ('computer', from 'computer / laptop / tablet') — flag it.

findings.criterion_text: Safety features described on the form are supported by the product page
findings.note: Safety-feature claims require manual comparison against the product page.

findings.criterion_text: Fee-match: listed price is consistent with the price on the form, and within the $1,500/budget-year cap
findings.note: Could not parse a fee from both the form and the website — please compare manually.

findings.criterion_text: HRI is approved in the participant's budget
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

findings.criterion_text: Item supports a goal in the participant's Life Plan
findings.note: Depends on internal records (budget / Life Plan / documents) — not checkable from the public web.

summary: Automated rule-based check of the provider's website found 0 of 4 website-verifiable items, with 3 needing manual review. No AI model was used for this review — a human should double-check any 'Needs Review' items before deciding.
